questions.question: Which port has become India’s first major port to receive the IGBC Platinum Rating for its administration building?
questions.explanation: V.O. Chidambaranar Port Authority (VOC Port) in Thoothukudi, Tamil Nadu, became India’s first major port to receive the prestigious IGBC Platinum Rating for its administration building in February 2026. The Indian Green Building Council (IGBC) awarded the highest Platinum certification in recognition of the port’s adoption of green, sustainable, and energy-efficient building practices. The administration building operates on 100% renewable energy, with 89% of its power generated on-site through solar energy and the remaining 11% sourced from other renewable energy sources.

questions.question: Which state government launched Sthree Suraksha Scheme to give monthly pension to unemployed women and transwomen?
questions.explanation: The Kerala government has launched the Sthree Suraksha Scheme to provide a monthly pension of ₹1,000 to unemployed women and transwomen. The first installment was transferred to 10,18,042 beneficiaries whose applications have been approved so far. Women aged between 35 and 60 years who are economically backward and not receiving any other social welfare pension are eligible for this scheme. Transwomen are also included as beneficiaries under this pension scheme.

questions.question: Which state has become the first legislature in India to use AI-powered face-recognition technology to mark MLA attendance?
questions.explanation: Andhra Pradesh introduced an Artificial Intelligence (AI) based face-recognition attendance system for Members of the Legislative Assembly (MLAs) in February 2026. The system was launched on 11 February 2026 at the beginning of the State Budget Session. Andhra Pradesh became the first State Legislature in India to use AI-powered face-recognition technology to mark MLA attendance. Nine AI-enabled cameras have been installed inside the Assembly to record attendance only when members sit in their allotted seats. The main objective is to prevent proxy attendance and ensure real participation in proceedings.

questions.question: Which country originally developed the Dornier 228 aircraft, recently seen in the news?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Defence has signed a ₹2,312 crore contract with Hindustan Aeronautics Limited to procure eight Dornier 228 aircraft for the Indian Coast Guard. The programme is expected to generate direct and indirect employment by strengthening HAL’s production ecosystem and supporting MSMEs and ancillary industries. It will create sustained opportunities in maintenance, repair, overhaul, and life-cycle technical support. Dornier 228 is a twin-engine turboprop Short Take-Off and Landing (STOL) utility aircraft for passenger, cargo, and special mission operations. Originally developed by Dornier GmbH (Germany) in the 1980s, it is now license-produced by HAL at its Kanpur facility.

questions.question: Which ministry has announced the launch of Bharat-VISTAAR, a multilingual AI tool for agriculture?
questions.explanation: The Finance Minister announced Bharat-VISTAAR, a multilingual AI tool to integrate AgriStack portals and ICAR’s agricultural practice package with AI systems. AgriStack is a Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) for the farm sector under the Digital Agriculture Mission (DAM), approved by the Union Cabinet in September 2024. AgriStack has three foundational registries: Farmer Registry, Crop Sown Registry, and Geo-referenced Village Maps, maintained by State Governments and Union Territories. Farmer Registry gives farmers a digital Farmer ID, linked to land records, livestock, crops, family, demographics, schemes, and benefits; target is 11 crore farmers.

questions.question: Designing Innovative Solutions for Holistic Access to Justice (DISHA) scheme is implemented by which ministry?
questions.explanation: The Government of India has undertaken multiple initiatives over the past five years to improve access to justice and fulfil its obligation under Article 39A of the Constitution, which mandates equal justice and free legal aid. The Department of Justice, Ministry of Law and Justice, is implementing the Designing Innovative Solutions for Holistic Access to Justice (DISHA) scheme with an outlay of ₹250 crore for 2021–2026 to provide a technology-driven, citizen-centric, pan-India access to justice framework. DISHA comprises three key components: Tele-Law, Nyaya Bandhu (Pro Bono Legal Services), and Legal Literacy & Legal Awareness Programmes, aimed at delivering inclusive and integrated legal support.

questions.question: Which institution has become the first in India to announce the launch of a face transplant programme?

options.label: A
options.text: All India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi

options.label: B
options.text: Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research, Chandigarh

options.label: C
options.text: Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education and Research, Puducherry

answer.label: A
answer.text: All India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi

questions.explanation: All India Institute of Medical Sciences, New Delhi became the first in India to announce a face transplant programme. The Department of Plastic, Reconstructive and Burns Surgery started a registry for patients with severe facial deformities and loss of functions like breathing and blinking. Face transplantation uses tissue from a deceased donor to restore facial function and appearance. Around 80 face transplants have been done worldwide, recently in Spain. Patients require lifelong immunosuppressive therapy to prevent rejection. The programme focuses on ethical screening, counselling and multidisciplinary support.

questions.question: Ol Chiki is the official script of which language?
questions.explanation: Ministry of Culture is organising the inaugural function of 100 years of Ol Chiki script on 16 February 2026 in New Delhi. Government of India is releasing a commemorative coin and postage stamp to mark the centenary. Ol Chiki script was developed in 1925 by Pandit Raghunath Murmu for Santhali language. Santhali was included in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution of India in 2003. The script has 30 letters designed to match Santhali phonetics accurately. Santhali is a tribal language of Austroasiatic family spoken in Jharkhand, Odisha, West Bengal, Assam and Bihar. The script helped standardise writing, education and literary growth of the language.

questions.question: Which country conducted its first air transport of a micro nuclear reactor using a C-17 military aircraft?
questions.explanation: The United States of America (USA) conducted its first air transport of a micro nuclear reactor using a C-17 military aircraft. The Departments of Energy (DOE) and Defense (DOD) transported the Ward microreactor developed by Valar Atomics from California to Hill Air Force Base without nuclear fuel. This shows rapid nuclear deployment capability for military and civilian use. The reactor can generate up to 5 megawatts, enough for about 5,000 homes. It will start at 100 kilowatts and gradually scale to full capacity.

questions.question: The Bhavya Bharat Bhushan Award was launched by which spiritual leader?
questions.explanation: Spiritual leader Sadhguru Jaggi Vasudev launched the Bhavya Bharat Bhushan Award. The award honours excellence in national security, business, arts and entertainment, sports, community welfare, science and technology, and literature and academia. It was presented during Mahashivaratri celebrations in the presence of Defence Minister Rajnath Singh. Violinist N. Rajam received the honour and called it a blessing. Former Indian Space Research Organisation Chairman S. Kiran Kumar said the award represents ISRO’s contribution to India’s space progress. The award aims to recognise individuals whose work significantly contributes to national growth and public well-being.
